original: Heavy pneumatic pillars brace either side of the boarding ramp, ready to haul it down to a planet’s surface for loading with cargo. It’s rated for loads as high as a Terran light tank - or an entire journey’s worth of supplies and spare parts.

Reaper Armaments helms are like their ships: designed for use in combat and little else. The arrangement of the control consoles bears out this philosophy. Weapons, shields, and sublight navigation are all convenient, redundant, and easy to find. Navigation is not. From the Captain’s strapped-in seat, you have to twist nearly ninety degrees to the side and stretch your arm to the limit to plot a course through the gate network.\n\n\t\tA reinforced bulkhead door offers access to the boarding ramp to the north. Storage access can be found through a flimsier southern door.

The secure storage terminal is the latest iteration on Reaper Armament’s “Auto-Quartermaster” project. Utilizing the latest in V.I. technology, this terminal automatically sorts deposited items into the most space-efficient arrangement. It even generates a detailed and tag-sortable manifest for quick withdrawals.

		The silvery door to the Captain’s quarters beckons to the west.
		
		A small workshop space is provided to the east.

The Captain’s Quarters: home away from home - a refuge from the inky black. The military fit comes with a single twin-sized bed. This civilian-issue model comes equipped with a much more luxurious queen size. Holos on the wall provide instantaneous updates on the entire vessel’s status, from position to fuel level to engine temperature. A planter on the other wall contains a line of basic-looking succulents, like a splash of green would somehow soften the utilitarian craft.

Though originally conceived as a military vessel, the Sledgehammer Mark II underwent a number of significant revisions for its presence on the civilian market: chief among them an onboard workshop. What better way to effect repairs than by fabricating your own parts? Without the benefit of military logistics, facilities like this are a necessity for lone Rushers. Larger items can be installed here. Storage access lies just to the west, and the Captain’s Quarters beyond that.

If someone had told you this security office was onboard a U.G.C. military vessel, you would have believed them. A small brig with two cells occupies the corner. A top-of-the-line security console sits against a wall, illuminated by the glow of a holographic security map with hardlight-controlled security feed projections. With just a tap, you can view any location on the ship from here.

		A heavily-reinforced airlock to the south guards against intrusion via boarding ramp.
		
		A small hatch leads down to the ship’s sole sub-level, where crew quarters and the mess are positioned. There’s no reason to go down there right now.

This central junction connects the ship’s aft corridors to the central accessway. Coolant pipes, power, and hydraulics follow the shaft’s turns. A heavy bulkhead door leads south, to the security office. The eastern and western branches delve deeper into the vessel’s aft, around its engines.

Bending with micron-accurate precision, the ship’s corridor’s fan out along the port engine housing in typical Reaper Armaments fashion - direct. This area was laid out with function over fashion in every inch. It’s ugly as hell with pipes and conduits going every which way, even under the grated deckplates.\n\n\t\tThe ship’s center lies west of here. The port engine access continues north and south.

Space is in short supply up here in the forward engine bay. Coolant pumps nestle against fuel injection systems while balanced above a tangle of hydraulics. Every step must be guarded against stubbed toes, and anyone taller than the average kaithrit male is going to have a bad time avoiding a gnarly noggin’ bonk.

Buried behind a cascade of neatly arranged wires and chubby conduits peeks a hint of faded pink. An older poster hangs there forgotten by its last owner. It features a supine, snake-"+({var0}.entryUnlocked("Caesselians")?"like Caesseli":"woman")+" lost in the throes of obvious sexual bliss. A very large and overly stylized Terran foot presses down on her sternum. The text reads, “Tread harder.” Getting back there to rip it down would require a significant rerouting of all these pipes, to say nothing of the bits of dodgy adhesive it would leave behind.

An older poster hangs there forgotten by its last owner. It features a supine, snake-"+(f.dI.entryUnlocked("Caesselians")?"like Caesseli":"woman")+" lost in the throes of obvious sexual bliss. A very large and overly stylized Terran foot presses down on her sternum. The text reads, “Tread harder.” Getting back there to rip it down would require a significant rerouting of all these pipes, to say nothing of the bits of dodgy adhesive it would leave behind.")

original: This aft passage follows the starboard-side reactor, engine, and associated subsystems. Well-positioned fire-suppression systems and magnetic tool racks hang vigilantly nearby. An immobilized ship is a dead ship, or so they say.\n\n\t\tAdditional access is offered to the north and south. To the east, the passage connects with the central corridor via a T-intersection.

Powerful engines rumble loudly enough for you to feel the vibrations through the deck below. The air tickles your nose with the acrid aroma of coolant mixed with old grease. This would be a cozy spot... for a galotian.\n\n\t\tThe rest of the engine bay lies south, where it connects to the rest of the ship proper.

The forward engine bay is cramped beyond measure. Pipes crowd at you from every angle. Hoses fill wrestle behind them like fibers of a drunken spider’s web. A flash of yellow can be spied deep within it all: a small sticker of a curled-up snake in a boot with the text, “No steppy snake!” underneath it.
